mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where the unemployment threshold is breached.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates robust aggregate demand that supports corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in GDP and retail sales fails to generate positive quarterly equity returns in over 60% of historical observations.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the index of industrial production during recessionary periods reflect severe supply-side shocks that immediately depress equity valuations over the following week.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines during recessions do not predict negative 5-day forward returns with a win rate above 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly CPI inflation exceeding 3.0 percent forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, which raises discount rates and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity markets produce positive 20-day returns in more than 65% of months where CPI exceeds the 3.0 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases household income and consumption capacity, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC for payroll growth remains below 0.01 or quarterly forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts during early-cycle recovery phases signal renewed capital investment and consumer confidence, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Housing starts increases during recovery phases fail to predict positive monthly equity returns with a win rate exceeding 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling retail sales indicates inventory buildup and weakening downstream demand, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following week.
failure_condition: The specified supply-demand divergence does not yield negative 5-day forward returns in at least 50% of observed instances over a 5-year sample.
